author: Arseniy Seroka
description: This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff. Opt-in publish individual notes or whole folders from Obsidian to a self-hosted Quartz site. Each URL is an unguessable random slug; sharing a file link reveals only that file, sharing a folder link reveals only its bundled notes.

manifest.author: Arseniy Seroka
manifest.description: Opt-in publish notes or whole folders to a self-hosted Quartz site. Each URL is an unguessable random slug; sharing a file link reveals only that file, sharing a folder link reveals only its bundled notes.
manifest.name: Private Quartz Publish

name: Private Quartz Publish

releases.author: jagajaga
releases.description:
Adds search to the published site and fixes a privacy leak in the reference server.

**Search (server-example)** — a small, theme-matched search bar on every published page:
- **In-page find** highlights matches and steps through them. Whitespace-flexible, so a phrase still matches when Markdown joined the words with a newline.
- **Folder search** (only inside a published folder bundle) searches the bundle's notes by file name and content. It reads only the current folder's index — no site-wide search, nothing new exposed. The stager emits a per-bundle `_search.json` consumed by the widget.

**Privacy fix (server-example)** — Quartz `--watch` never deletes output HTML when its source Markdown disappears, so an unpublished or slug-rotated page would keep serving forever. The build now sweeps any HTML whose source no longer exists and watches the content dir so unpublishes propagate to the live site within ~0.5s.

Plugin binary unchanged from v1.0.7 — these changes are in the self-hosting reference server (`server-example/`).
releases.name: 1.0.8 — in-page & folder search + stale-URL leak fix

releases.author: jagajaga
releases.description:
Fixes the two issues reported by the community.obsidian.md automated review.

**Error fixed:** Plugin description no longer contains the word "Obsidian". The directory implies it by context.
**Warning fixed:** Replaced the `builtin-modules` package with Node's built-in `module.builtinModules` export. Same behavior, one fewer dependency.

Plugin behavior unchanged from v1.0.6.

Also: README now has a hero screenshot of a folder bundle with the hierarchical sidebar visible.
releases.name: 1.0.7 — community directory fixes

releases.author: jagajaga
releases.description:
**Server-side fix.** The plugin itself is unchanged from v1.0.5; this release reuploads main.js + manifest.json for completeness.

If a published note has `folder_slug` + `folder_name` in frontmatter but no `folder_path` (older plugin builds — v1.0.3 and below), the stager now derives the folder_path by locating the folder_name in the note's vault path. This means users whose devices haven't pulled the latest plugin yet still get hierarchical sidebars instead of flat lists.

No change required if you already updated to v1.0.5 — both code paths give the same output.
releases.name: v1.0.6 — hierarchical sidebar for older plugin versions

releases.author: jagajaga
releases.description: Obsidian-spec-compliant tag (no v prefix) for the same release as v1.0.6. See https://github.com/jagajaga/private-quartz-publish/releases/tag/v1.0.6 for full notes.

releases.author: jagajaga
releases.description:
Polish pass before submitting to the Obsidian community plugin marketplace.

**Plugin code changes (all internal — no UX change):**
- Replaced `any` types in menu callbacks with the proper Menu / MenuItem types from obsidian
- Replaced `window.confirm()` with a styled Modal subclass for folder publish/unpublish prompts
- Removed plugin-injected <h2>/<h3> elements in settings tab; use Setting().setHeading() per Obsidian style guide

**Server stack — much easier to deploy:**
- docker-compose now bundles Caddy alongside stager + quartz. One `docker compose up` brings up the entire stack with automatic Let's Encrypt HTTPS.
- New `setup.sh` wizard prompts for vault path + public domain, writes .env, pulls images from GHCR, brings the stack up.
- Pre-built docker images pushed to GHCR via GitHub Actions on every release — no local build required.

**README:**
- Upfront warning that the plugin requires a self-hosted server (so the audience self-selects).
- Quick-start uses `./setup.sh` as the primary path.
releases.name: v1.0.5 — polish for community plugin submission

releases.author: jagajaga
releases.description:
When a published folder contains subfolders, the sidebar now renders them as nested groups instead of a flat list.

```
HierarchyTest
├─ 2025/
│  ├─ Q1/
│  │  └─ note B
│  └─ note A
├─ 2026/
│  └─ note C
└─ Intro
```

Folders sort before files at every level; both alphabetical with natural number ordering.

**Plugin change:** publishing a folder now writes a `folder_path` frontmatter field on each note recording its position within the bundle.
**Server side:** the stager preserves `folder_path` in the staged copy (and falls back to computing it from the vault path for legacy / data.json-only bundles). The FolderSidebar component builds a tree from these values.

Existing bundle URLs keep working.
releases.name: v1.0.4 — hierarchical folder sidebar

releases.author: jagajaga
releases.description:
**Publish folder** now walks the entire subtree, not just direct children. Right-click a folder `A` containing subfolders `A/sub1`, `A/sub2`, etc. — every `.md` anywhere under `A` is published in one go, all under the same folder URL with one shared sidebar.

**Stable URLs on re-publish.** If you re-publish a folder, the plugin reuses the existing folder slug (taken from any descendant's frontmatter or the local data.json cache) instead of minting a new one. Previously shared folder links stay valid.

**Unpublish folder** is correspondingly recursive — unpublishes every `.md` descendant.

No server-side changes required; the stager already groups by `folder_slug` regardless of depth.
releases.name: v1.0.3 — recursive folder publish

releases.author: jagajaga
releases.description:
Fixes a display bug where published pages showed the random slug as the page title instead of the original note name.

Server-side fix in `server-example/stager/main.ts` — the plugin itself is unchanged from v1.0.1, but the bundled release also ships the latest plugin artifacts.

If you only run the published plugin (no server-side changes), upgrading does nothing. The fix is to redeploy the stager: `cd server-example && docker compose up -d --build stager`.
releases.name: v1.0.2 — show real filename as page title

releases.author: jagajaga
releases.description:
Folder publish state now lives in each child note's frontmatter (`folder_slug`, `folder_name`) instead of only in the plugin's `data.json`. This fixes a bug where folder URLs returned 404 on multi-device setups where the vault sync mechanism (e.g. Self-hosted LiveSync's 'Sync hidden files') excludes plugin data files from replication.

**Install (manually):**
1. Download `main.js` and `manifest.json` below.
2. Drop them into `<your-vault>/.obsidian/plugins/private-quartz-publish/`.
3. Obsidian → Settings → Community plugins → enable **Private Quartz Publish**.
4. Open the plugin settings and set your **Base URL**.

**Install (via BRAT):** add `jagajaga/private-quartz-publish` as a beta plugin.

See [README](https://github.com/jagajaga/private-quartz-publish#readme) for the full server setup.
releases.name: v1.0.1 — sync-resilient folder publish
